codeReview.summary: The commit usefully centralizes paused/archived-company admission messaging and covers several entrypoints, but it still leaves one lifecycle restart path unguarded. A deferred issue-execution wake can be promoted into a brand-new run after the company has been paused or archived, so the quiesce/admission guarantee is not actually enforced in every path. The intent-queue gate is also still fragmented across callers instead of being enforced at the shared primitive.

issues.description: `releaseIssueExecutionAndPromote()` clears the current issue execution lock and then promotes the oldest `deferred_issue_execution` request without checking the company's lifecycle state. In this commit the loop only rejects non-invokable agents (lines 3323-3339) before inserting a fresh queued run (lines 3368-3381), so pausing or archiving a company while a run is active can still restart work as soon as that run is cancelled/finalized. That violates the feature's requirement that paused/archived companies stop admitting new work across lifecycle-admission paths.

`intentQueue.createIntent()` still has no company-admission check, so the lifecycle gate is only enforced in selected callers instead of at the shared intent-creation primitive. The reviewed commit adds caller-side guards in some producers, but any direct `createIntent()` caller can still enqueue new work for a paused or archived company unless it remembers to duplicate the guard first.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `backend-worker` skill's unconditional 'write tests first' step does not fit dirty-worktree continuation features like this one, where the worker is explicitly told to adopt and integrate an existing in-flight batch before making coherent fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md` step 3 requires 'Write tests first', while the feature preconditions in `features.json` explicitly say to continue the dirty lifecycle batch and the handoff's `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is false for that reason.

summary: Review failed: the commit leaves deferred issue-execution promotion able to restart work after a company has been paused/archived, so lifecycle admission is still not fully stabilized. I also noted a skill-procedure gap for dirty-worktree continuation features.
